VAULTCHEF

Date Night: Citrus Glow Italian Night

Low-effort Italian-inspired menu with bright lemon, herbs, and creamy finishes

Saturday, February 14, 2026

    Music Pairing

    Artist: Paul Desmond

    Music Pairing

    Album: Bossa Antigua

    Artist: Paul Desmond

    Style: Instrumental jazz (cool/bossa)

    Sparkling Basil Limonata

    Ingredients

    • 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice
    • 2 tbsp honey or simple syrup
    • 8 fresh basil leaves, plus extra to garnish
    • 1 cup chilled apple juice
    • 1 cup chilled soda water
    • Ice cubes
    • 2 lemon wheels (for garnish)

    Method

    1. In a jug, muddle basil leaves with honey and lemon juice for 20-30 seconds.
    2. Strain into the jug again for a smoother drink, pressing well to keep the basil flavor.
    3. Add apple juice and stir until combined.
    4. Fill two glasses with ice and divide the mixture evenly.
    5. Top each glass with soda water, stir gently, and garnish with a lemon wheel and basil leaf.
    6. Optional cocktail conversion for one serving: add 30 ml (1 oz) gin or vodka to one glass only, then stir once.

    Whipped Feta Artichoke Toasts with Lemon

    Ingredients

    • 120 g feta
    • 80 g ricotta
    • 1 tsp lemon zest
    • 1 tbsp lemon juice
    • 1 small garlic clove, finely grated
    • 1 tbsp olive oil, plus extra to drizzle
    • 1 cup jarred artichoke hearts, drained and chopped
    • 1 tbsp chopped parsley or basil
    • 4 slices sourdough
    • Freshly cracked black pepper

    Method

    1. Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
    2. In a bowl, mash feta, ricotta, lemon zest, and half the lemon juice until creamy and spreadable.
    3. Warm olive oil in a small pan over medium heat, then cook artichokes with garlic for 2-3 minutes.
    4. Add remaining lemon juice and parsley, toss for 30 seconds, then turn off the heat.
    5. Spread whipped feta on the toasts, spoon warm artichokes over the top, and finish with black pepper and a light olive oil drizzle.

    One-Pan Lemon Parmesan Gnocchi with Spinach

    Ingredients

    • 500 g shelf-stable potato gnocchi
    • 1 tbsp olive oil
    • 30 g butter
    • 8-10 fresh sage leaves
    • 2 garlic cloves, minced
    • 1 cup low-sodium vegetable stock
    • 3/4 cup thickened cream
    • 1 lemon (zest + 2 tbsp juice)
    • 1/2 cup finely grated parmesan, plus extra to finish
    • 2 cups baby spinach
    • Salt and black pepper
    • Optional: pinch of chili flakes

    Method

    1. Heat a large frying pan over medium heat with butter until foamy, add sage leaves, and fry for 30-45 seconds until crisp; transfer leaves to a plate.
    2. Add olive oil and gnocchi to the same pan and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly golden.
    3. Add garlic (and chili flakes if using) and stir for 30 seconds.
    4. Pour in stock and cream, then simmer for 3-4 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
    5. Stir in lemon zest, lemon juice, parmesan, and spinach; cook for 1-2 minutes until spinach wilts and sauce is glossy.
    6. Season to taste, divide between bowls, and top with crispy sage, extra parmesan, and black pepper.

    No-Bake Lemon Mascarpone Pots with Pistachio Crunch

    Ingredients

    • 125 g mascarpone
    • 150 ml thickened cream
    • 2 tbsp icing sugar
    • 1 tsp vanilla extract
    • 1 tsp finely grated lemon zest
    • 1 tbsp lemon juice
    • 6 plain sweet biscuits (e.g., Marie biscuits), crushed
    • 2 tbsp chopped pistachios
    • Pinch of flaky salt
    • Optional: extra lemon zest to finish

    Method

    1. Mix crushed biscuits, pistachios, and flaky salt in a small bowl.
    2. Whip cream, icing sugar, and vanilla to soft peaks.
    3. Fold mascarpone, lemon zest, and lemon juice into the whipped cream until smooth.
    4. Spoon a little biscuit mixture into two small glasses, then add a thick layer of lemon mascarpone cream.
    5. Repeat with another light biscuit layer and finish with cream on top.
    6. Chill for 20 minutes, then garnish with extra lemon zest before serving.
